Durio zibethinus is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the durian tree. The health benefits of Durio zibethinus include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Durio zibethinus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Durio zibethinus include durian oil, durian seed extract, and durian fruit powder, which are used in traditional medicine to support digestive health and immune function.
taxonomy: Durabaculum kajewskii is a genus of flowering plants in the family Equisetaceae. It is a type of horsetail, a vascular plant that has been around for over 360 million years. Durabaculum kajewskii is closely related to the genus Equisetum, which includes the more common horsetail species. The classification of Durabaculum kajewskii is still a subject of study, but it is generally placed in the family Equisetaceae, order Equisetales, and class Equisetopsida. Further research is needed to confirm its exact classification and relationships within the plant kingdom.
